---
title: "Final Year Project Management System (PFE)"
subtitle: "Final Year Project Report"
author: "HADID Rami"
date: last-modified
lang: en
csl: norms/ieee.csl
bibliography: bibliography/references.bib

format:
  html:
    css:             styles.css
    toc:             true
    lof:             true
    theme:           cosmo
    number-sections: true
  pdf:
    title-block:     false
    documentclass: report
    classoption: [a4paper, 12pt]
    geometry:
      - top=2.3cm
      - bottom=2.3cm
      - left=2.3cm
      - right=2.3cm
    include-in-header: config/header.tex
    include-before-body: config/layout.tex
    toc: true
    lof: false
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: black
    urlcolor: blue
    citecolor: black
    filecolor: black
    fig-pos: htbp
---


```{=latex}
\clearpage
\listoffigures
```

{{< include chapters/intro/main.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/ch1/main.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/ch2/main.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/ch3/main.qmd >}}

{{< include chapters/ch4/main.qmd >}}

# General Conclusion and Perspectives {.unnumbered}

\markboth{General Conclusion and Perspectives}{}

This thesis addressed the problem of managing Final Year Projects (PFE) within Algerian
university institutions, following a progressive approach from the analysis of the existing
context through to the delivery of an operational solution.

The first chapter established the theoretical framework by situating the PFE within the
Algerian educational system and identifying the recurring dysfunctions in its current
management: informal coordination, lack of traceability, and information loss.
The second chapter confirmed these findings through a comparative analysis of existing
platforms and a case study conducted at the ESST in Algiers, demonstrating the absence of
a solution adapted to the Algerian university context.

Drawing on these findings, the third chapter presented the complete system design by
defining the functional requirements of each actor and formalising the expected behaviour
through rigorous UML modelling. The fourth chapter translated this design into reality by
detailing the technology choices made (SvelteKit for the frontend, Go/Fiber for the backend,
and SQLite for persistence) and presenting the developed interfaces covering the entire PFE
lifecycle.

The resulting solution meets the identified requirements: it offers a unified platform
covering topic proposal, expression of preferences, assignment, pedagogical supervision,
defence scheduling, and grading. Its lightweight architecture and dependency-free deployment
make it particularly well suited to the constraints of Algerian university institutions.

As future directions, several areas for improvement can be envisioned: the integration of
a real-time notification system, the automatic generation of administrative documents
(defence minutes, certificates), the addition of an advanced statistical analysis module
for decision-making, and the extension of the application to other institutions via a
multi-tenant architecture.



# Bibliography {.unnumbered}

\markboth{Bibliography}{}

::: {#refs}
:::
